text:
There once was a garden gnome named Harold. He lived alone in a cozy little village where everybody knew each other after years of peaceful community life. Every day, Harold would trompFH around his patch of flowers, collecting sunbeams, munching on daisies, and occasionally humming a sweet, little tune.

One autumn morning, Harold noticed something unusual. Instead of the usual parade of birds darting through his garden, only four little squirrels crowded around him. They nibbled on his tulips with a greediness that made Harold quite annoyed.

"Who dares eat my precious tulips?" he huffed.

The squirrels looked up sheepishly.

"I'm sorry, Harold. We're just very hungry," said the oldest. "We heard you make the tastiest nuts. We thought, why not?"

Harold rolled his eyes. "Fine. But remember these nuts are mine!"

Over the weeks, his garden grew sparse. Every time Harold thought about nut-collecting
